title: Litobrochia vespertilionis
intro:
There is no information available on the plant Litobrochia vespertilionis, however based on available information the plant Litobrochia vespertilionis is an Ascomycete fungus. 

Litobrochia vespertilionis, an Ascomycete fungus, is often used in traditional medicine due to its numerous health benefits, including antimicrobial and antifungal properties that may aid in treating various infections and promoting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Litobrochia vespertilionis include sesquiterpenoids and other secondary metabolites that contribute to its medicinal properties. Litobrochia vespertilionis is traditionally used to prepare tinctures and extracts, which are used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Litobrochia vespertilionis is a genus of moths within the family Erebidae. It belongs to the order Lepidoptera, which comprises butterflies and moths. Further classification places Litobrochia vespertilionis within the family Erebidae and the superfamily Noctuoidea. The genus Litobrochia is also part of the subfamily Erebinae. This classification is based on the biological characteristics and evolutionary relationships of the species.
